title: hydrangea scandens
intro: Hydrangea scandens, also known as climbing hydrangea, is a type of flowering vine native to eastern Asia, characterized by its large white or pink flowers and ability to climb up trees and other supports. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, including wounds, fever, and rheumatism. The health benefits of hydrangea scandens include its ability to reduce swelling, soothe sore throats, and calm digestive issues. Herbal preparations of hydrangea scandens are often made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
